name: From Fuendetodos to the Torcas Reservoir - return via the Cariñena Field
name_original: De Fuendetodos hasta el Pantano de Torcas - vuelta por el Campo de Cariñena
region_intro: Road cycling around Fuendetodos offers diverse terrain characterized by Mediterranean scrubland, extensive Alepo pine forests, and holm oaks. The region features rolling hills and dramatic deep gorges carved into Jurassic-era limestone rocks, providing varied topography. These natural features create engaging rides with both ascents and descents. The area also includes historic 18th-century stone neveras, adding a cultural dimension to the routes. Best road cycling routes in Fuendetodos The most popular road cycling route is Zaragoza - Herrera de los Navarros - Zaragoza — circular by Fuendetodos and Virgen de la Doloreta, a 92.6 miles (149.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views across the varied landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Fuendetodos to the Torcas Reservoir - return via the Cariñena Field, a moderate 24.9 miles (40.1 km) path. This route traverses rolling hills and offers views of the Torcas Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the View of Cadrete Castle – Virgen de Dorleta Memorial loop from Rio Huerva, a 69.9 miles (112.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of Cadrete Castle, often completed in about 5 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Fuendetodos is defined by rolling hills, pine forests, and dramatic gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Fuendetodos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuendetodos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Fuendetodos?
A: The terrain around Fuendetodos is diverse, featuring Mediterranean scrubland, extensive Alepo pine forests, and holm oaks. Road cyclists can expect a mix of rolling hills and dramatic deep gorges carved into Jurassic-era limestone rocks. This varied topography provides engaging rides with both ascents and descents, offering a range of challenges.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in the Fuendetodos area?
A: Road cycling routes in Fuendetodos cater to various skill levels. While there are no easy routes listed, you'll find 3 moderate routes and 9 difficult routes. This means the area is well-suited for cyclists looking for a good challenge and rewarding climbs.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or natural features to see while road cycling near Fuendetodos?
A: Yes, the region is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past unique 18th-century stone &lt;em&gt;neveras&lt;/em&gt; (ice houses), which are fascinating remnants of the region's past. The dramatic gorges formed by ancient glaciers also offer stunning visual experiences. Additionally, routes may pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de Dorleta Memorial&lt;/a&gt; or offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints on road cycling routes around Fuendetodos?
A: Absolutely. The varied landscape of Fuendetodos, with its rolling hills, pine forests, and deep gorges, naturally offers many scenic vistas. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988448558/zaragoza-herrera-de-los-navarros-zaragoza-circular-by-fuendetodos-and-virgen-de-la-doloreta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaragoza - Herrera de los Navarros - Zaragoza&lt;/a&gt; circular route provide extensive views across the diverse landscape. You can also find specific viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for circular road cycling routes in Fuendetodos?
A: Yes, several circular road cycling routes are available. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988448558/zaragoza-herrera-de-los-navarros-zaragoza-circular-by-fuendetodos-and-virgen-de-la-doloreta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaragoza - Herrera de los Navarros - Zaragoza — circular by Fuendetodos and Virgen de la Doloreta&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 149 km loop. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7801466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cadrete Castle – Virgen de Dorleta Memorial loop from Rio Huerva&lt;/a&gt;, covering 112.4 km.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes starting from or passing through Fuendetodos?
A: Yes, Fuendetodos is part of several longer road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988448558/zaragoza-herrera-de-los-navarros-zaragoza-circular-by-fuendetodos-and-virgen-de-la-doloreta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaragoza - Herrera de los Navarros - Zaragoza&lt;/a&gt; route is a substantial 149 km ride. Another long-distance option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31879617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cadrete Castle – Virgen de Dorleta Memorial loop from Zaragoza-Goya&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 127 km.

Q: Is public transport available to Fuendetodos for cyclists, especially from Zaragoza?
A: While specific public transport options for bikes directly to Fuendetodos are not detailed, the region is well-connected by road. Routes often originate from Zaragoza, suggesting that reaching the area by car with bikes is a common approach. For detailed public transport information, especially regarding bike carriage, it's advisable to check local bus services or regional train lines connecting Zaragoza to nearby towns.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes in Fuendetodos?
A: Fuendetodos itself is a small town, and while specific cafes along every route are not listed, it's a good practice to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer rides. Larger towns like Zaragoza, which many routes pass through or start from, will offer ample refreshment opportunities. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially when exploring the more remote sections of the routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Fuendetodos?
A: The road cycling routes around Fuendetodos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied landscape, which includes rolling hills, pine forests, and dramatic gorges, providing a stimulating and visually rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Fuendetodos?
A: The Aragon region, where Fuendetodos is located, generally experiences hot summers and cold winters. Therefore, the best time for road cycling is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more pleasant for longer rides, allowing you to fully enjoy the natural beauty and historical sites without extreme temperatures.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options around Fuendetodos?
A: The road cycling routes around Fuendetodos are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no easy routes specifically listed. This suggests that most routes might be more suitable for experienced cyclists or families with older, more capable riders. For families seeking gentler rides, it would be advisable to look for shorter, less hilly sections or explore the extensive network of footpaths that might have suitable connecting roads.
tips: Tosos is a small town located on the banks of the Huerva river. The alluvial terraces formed by the passage of the river have created natural differences where the inhabitants of the area take advantage of this by planting fruit trees typical of these dry spaces.
Near the town of Tosos and Aguilón we find ourselves in the dam with wonderful views of the Torcas Reservoir that is part of the Huerva River.
La Casaza, is a fortified watchtower, located on the Torcas Reservoir, in the Zaragoza municipality of Tosos, 2 km from the town and a few kilometers from the Torcas reservoir.
